title: IMO GHG Strategy 2023
note: Net-zero by ~2050. Checkpoints: 20% by 2030, 70% by 2040. Replaces 2018 strategy.
reasoning: Sets the global trajectory for carrier fleet investment and green fuel adoption — every ocean freight contract is shaped by this.
whatIsIt: IMO's revised strategy committing global shipping to net-zero GHG emissions by around 2050 with interim checkpoints.
whyMatters: This strategy determines carrier fleet investment direction over the next 25 years. Freight forwarders must understand which carriers are investing ahead of the curve to negotiate favorable long-term contracts. Green fuel surcharges will be directly linked to these targets.

title: FuelEU Maritime
note: GHG intensity limits: 2% by 2025, 80% by 2050. Penalty €2,400/tonne VLSFO shortfall.
reasoning: Mandatory GHG intensity limits with €2,400/tonne penalties — direct fuel surcharge driver.
whatIsIt: EU mandate requiring GHG intensity of energy used onboard ships at EU ports to decrease progressively.
whyMatters: Carriers will pass through fuel compliance costs on every EU port call. High-value cargo operators must budget for escalating surcharges and compare green corridor premiums against conventional routing costs.
keyData: Applies to all EU port calls

title: EU ETS for Shipping
note: Ships >5,000GT surrender ETS allowances: 40%(2024), 70%(2025), 100%(2026).
reasoning: Directly binding ETS obligation with financial penalties. Immediate cost pass-through on every EU shipment.
whatIsIt: EU regulation extending the Emissions Trading System to maritime shipping for vessels over 5,000 gross tonnage.
whyMatters: Every ocean shipment via EU ports faces ETS surcharges passed through by carriers. Event logistics specialists must budget for escalating costs (40%→100%) and factor ETS into lane comparisons when routing equipment and staging materials.
keyData: Applies to vessels >5,000 GT calling EU ports

title: CII (Carbon Intensity Indicator)
note: Annual A-E rating; D for 3yrs or E for 1yr triggers corrective action. MEPC 83 completed Phase 1 review; Z-factors set for 2027-2030.
reasoning: Annual vessel ratings directly affect carrier fleet viability and charter market pricing. Phase 1 review complete — thresholds confirmed tightening.
whatIsIt: IMO's operational carbon intensity rating system assigning annual A-E grades to individual vessels. MEPC 83 completed Phase 1 review and set CII reduction (Z) factors for 2027-2030.
whyMatters: Vessels rated D or E face operational restrictions and corrective action requirements. Freight forwarders should verify carrier vessel CII ratings when booking — poorly rated vessels may face speed restrictions that affect transit times for time-sensitive event cargo. Z-factors for 2027-2030 now confirmed means predictable tightening schedule.
keyData: D for 3 years or E for 1 year → corrective action plan

title: IMO MARPOL Annex VI
note: Sulphur cap 0.5% global, 0.1% ECA zones. NOx Tier III.
reasoning: Sulphur and NOx limits drive fuel choices and scrubber surcharges across all ocean routes.
whatIsIt: International maritime air pollution rules setting limits on sulphur oxides, nitrogen oxides, and particulate matter from ships.
whyMatters: Sulphur compliance costs are embedded in every ocean freight rate. ECA zone surcharges apply on Northern European and North American routes frequently used for event equipment shipping.
keyData: NOx Tier III for new engines
Compliance via low-sulphur fuel or scrubbers

title: EU MRV Regulation
note: Mandatory CO2 monitoring/reporting/verification for ships >5,000GT at EU ports.
reasoning: Mandatory compliance portal — all EU ETS maritime data flows through THETIS-MRV.
whatIsIt: EU regulation requiring monitoring, reporting and verification of CO2 emissions for large ships calling at EU ports.
whyMatters: Vessel-level emissions data determines carrier ETS exposure and surcharge legitimacy. Freight forwarders can verify carrier environmental claims and identify lowest-emission vessels for client sustainability reports.

title: Getting to Zero Coalition
note: Industry alliance targeting zero-emission vessels by 2030.
reasoning: Green corridor pilots on major trade lanes inform surcharge viability by route.
whatIsIt: Industry coalition of 200+ companies working to deploy commercially viable zero-emission vessels by 2030.
whyMatters: Corridor pilots on routes like EU-Asia and transatlantic directly affect lane options for all freight sectors. Understanding pilot timelines helps price green shipping premiums for clients requesting low-carbon logistics.

title: Alternative Fuels Insight (IRENA/IMO)
note: Tracks LNG, methanol, ammonia, hydrogen adoption across global fleet.
reasoning: Fuel transition data essential for understanding which carriers are future-proofing fleets.
whatIsIt: IRENA's tracking of alternative fuel adoption rates and technology readiness across the global shipping fleet.
whyMatters: Fuel transition rates indicate which carriers are investing in compliance. Event logistics specialists can identify forward-looking carrier partners and avoid those facing stranded asset risk.

title: Norway Zero-Emission Shipping
note: World's first zero-emission fjord requirements by 2026.
reasoning: Norway leads with mandatory zero-emission requirements — signals direction for other jurisdictions.
whatIsIt: Norway's pioneering regulations requiring zero-emission vessel operations in Norwegian fjords from 2026.
whyMatters: Norway's requirements signal the direction for other jurisdictions. Freight forwarders routing through Scandinavian ports should monitor for compliance implications on vessel selection.
keyData: World's first mandatory ZEV shipping zone
Applies to domestic ferry and cargo routes

title: ESPO (European Sea Ports Organisation)
note: EU port sustainability standards, shore power, cold ironing.
reasoning: Port sustainability standards affect dwell costs and operational requirements at EU hubs.
whatIsIt: European port industry body setting sustainability standards and tracking shore power deployment.
whyMatters: Port-level requirements (shore power mandates, emission zones) affect vessel selection and dwell costs at major European hubs used for event equipment import/export.

title: Lloyd's Register Decarbonisation Hub
note: Technical guidance on fleet decarbonisation pathways.
reasoning: Classification society guidance shapes carrier fleet investment decisions and fuel transition timelines.
whatIsIt: Lloyd's Register's technical advisory platform for maritime fleet decarbonisation pathways and fuel transition.
whyMatters: Classification society guidance directly influences carrier fleet decisions. Understanding LR's recommended pathways helps freight forwarders assess carrier readiness and negotiate informed contracts.

title: Blue Visby Solution
note: Speed optimisation reducing voyage emissions 10-15%.
reasoning: Operational efficiency tool — immediate emissions reduction without hardware changes.
whatIsIt: Digital platform optimising vessel speed profiles to reduce voyage emissions by 10-15% without hardware modifications.
whyMatters: Speed optimisation tools offer immediate emissions reductions for ocean freight. Forwarders can ask carriers about Blue Visby or similar tools when clients request lower-carbon shipping options.

title: CORSIA (ICAO)
note: Carbon offsetting for international aviation. Mandatory from 2027.
reasoning: Global offsetting costs embedded in air cargo rates from 2027 mandatory phase.
whatIsIt: ICAO's Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation — a global market-based measure for international flights.
whyMatters: CORSIA offset costs flow through to air cargo rates on international routes. Freight forwarders must factor offset costs into air freight quotes, especially for time-critical and high-value cargo.

title: EU Aviation ETS
note: Aviation in EU ETS since 2012. Free allowances out by 2026. Scope expanding.
reasoning: Intra-EEA carbon costs already in rates; scope expansion would transform transatlantic air cargo pricing.
whatIsIt: EU Emissions Trading System applied to aviation — currently intra-EEA flights, expanding to 50% of international flights from 2027.
whyMatters: All intra-European air freight already carries ETS carbon cost. Scope expansion to international flights would significantly increase transatlantic and EU-Asia air cargo rates across all sectors.

title: ReFuelEU Aviation
note: SAF blending: 2% by 2025, 6% by 2030, 70% by 2050. All EU airports.
reasoning: Mandatory SAF blend directly increases air cargo fuel surcharges at all EU airports.
whatIsIt: EU regulation mandating minimum Sustainable Aviation Fuel blending percentages for all fuel uplifted at EU airports.
whyMatters: Every air cargo uplift at EU airports carries a SAF cost premium. Event logistics operators must pass through SAF surcharges and compare EU vs non-EU routing costs for time-sensitive show freight.
keyData: Applies to ALL fuel uplifted at EU airports

title: UK SAF Mandate
reasoning: Independent UK mandate means separate SAF surcharge layer for UK-departing event freight.
whatIsIt: UK's independent Sustainable Aviation Fuel mandate applying to all UK-departing flights.
whyMatters: Post-Brexit UK has its own SAF mandate with different targets than the EU. Freight forwarders routing through UK airports face a separate surcharge structure — important for London-based event logistics.

title: SAFA (Sustainable Air Freight Alliance)
note: Industry body promoting SAF adoption for air cargo.
reasoning: Industry coalition driving SAF procurement — member commitments affect tender requirements.
whatIsIt: Industry alliance promoting SAF adoption specifically for air cargo operations and Scope 3 accounting.
whyMatters: SAFA member companies increasingly require SAF usage in cargo tenders. Understanding alliance commitments helps freight forwarders anticipate client requirements for sustainable air logistics.

title: ICAO Carbon Calculator
note: Official methodology for aviation CO2 per cargo tonne.
reasoning: Standard calculator for air freight emissions — baseline for client reporting.
whatIsIt: ICAO's official methodology and calculator for quantifying aviation CO2 emissions per passenger or cargo tonne-kilometre.
whyMatters: Standard reference for air freight emissions calculations. Freight forwarders use this as baseline methodology when responding to client emissions data requests for air cargo shipments.

title: GLEC Framework (Air Freight)
note: ISO 14083-aligned air freight emissions accounting.
reasoning: Industry standard methodology for logistics emissions — required in client tenders.
whatIsIt: Smart Freight Centre's Global Logistics Emissions Council framework for calculating air freight emissions, aligned with ISO 14083.
whyMatters: GLEC is the methodology event logistics specialists actually use for shipment-level air freight emissions. Clients increasingly specify GLEC-compliant reporting in RFPs and tenders.

title: Aviation Week: Sustainability
note: Industry news on SAF, fleet transitions, carrier sustainability.
reasoning: Industry intelligence on carrier sustainability moves — informs carrier selection.
whatIsIt: Leading aviation industry publication covering SAF developments, fleet transitions, and carrier sustainability programmes.
whyMatters: Trade press intelligence on which carriers are investing in SAF, ordering efficient aircraft, and setting sustainability targets — useful for carrier selection and client advisory.

title: EU CO2 Standards for Heavy Trucks
reasoning: Aggressive truck CO2 targets will reshape European road freight fleet composition and costs.
whatIsIt: EU regulation setting CO2 emission performance standards for new heavy-duty vehicles with declining targets to 2040.
whyMatters: These targets determine when diesel trucks are phased out of European fleets. Freight forwarders relying on road transport for last-mile delivery and drayage must plan for fleet transition costs from subcontractors.
keyData: Affects all EU road freight subcontractors

title: Euro 7 Standard
note: New emission limits for trucks. Lower NOx/particulate thresholds.
reasoning: New pollutant limits affect drayage fleet procurement and LEZ access across EU.
whatIsIt: EU regulation setting new pollutant emission limits for vehicles including heavy-duty trucks, tightening NOx and particulate standards.
whyMatters: Non-compliant vehicles face Low Emission Zone restrictions across European cities — critical for last-mile event freight delivery. Subcontractor fleet compliance must be verified.
keyData: Tightens NOx by ~56% vs Euro VI
Includes brake and tyre particle emissions

title: EU AFIR (Alt Fuels Infrastructure)
note: Mandatory EV charging + H2 refuelling every 60km on TEN-T by 2025-2027.
reasoning: Infrastructure rollout determines ZEV fleet feasibility for European road freight.
whatIsIt: EU regulation mandating deployment of EV charging and hydrogen refuelling infrastructure along the trans-European transport network.
whyMatters: Infrastructure availability determines whether zero-emission road freight is viable on specific corridors. Forwarders must track rollout to assess which routes can support electric trucks.
keyData: EV charging every 60km on TEN-T core by 2025

title: CER (European Railways)
note: EU rail decarbonisation, electrification targets, modal shift.
reasoning: Rail modal shift economics directly relevant when clients ask for lower-carbon alternatives.
whatIsIt: Community of European Railways promoting rail decarbonisation and modal shift from road to rail.
whyMatters: Rail offers significantly lower emissions per tonne-km than road. When clients request green logistics options, rail alternatives for event equipment can reduce carbon footprint by 60-80%.
keyData: Rail produces ~75% less CO2 than road per tkm

title: European Clean Trucking Alliance
note: Advocacy for rapid ZEV truck adoption in Europe.
reasoning: Tracks EU legislative progress on heavy-duty vehicle standards.
whatIsIt: Advocacy coalition pushing for accelerated zero-emission truck adoption across Europe.
whyMatters: Policy signals from this alliance indicate direction of EU truck regulations. Useful for anticipating fleet transition timelines.

title: EPA Heavy-Duty Phase 3 Rule
note: GHG standards for MY2027-2032; ~60% new sleeper cabs ZEV by 2032.
reasoning: Stringent US truck standards under political uncertainty — affects drayage and interstate fleet planning.
whatIsIt: EPA's stringent GHG performance standards for heavy-duty trucks covering model years 2027-2032.
whyMatters: These standards determine the US truck fleet transition timeline. Freight forwarders with US operations must plan for drayage fleet changes, especially at West Coast ports handling event equipment imports.
keyData: ~60% of new sleeper cabs ZEV by 2032

title: CARB Advanced Clean Trucks
note: California: 55% Class 4-8 ZEV sales by 2035. 12 states following.
reasoning: CARB rules control US West Coast port drayage — non-compliance means no port access.
whatIsIt: California Air Resources Board mandate requiring increasing percentages of new truck sales to be zero-emission.
whyMatters: All drayage at LA/Long Beach — the primary US gateway for event equipment — must comply. Non-compliant trucks face port access restrictions. 12+ states follow California's rules.

title: Drive Electric: Zero-Emission Freight
note: US federal ZEV infrastructure funding and grant programmes.
reasoning: Federal funding programmes for fleet electrification — relevant for US subcontractor readiness.
whatIsIt: US federal government portal for zero-emission vehicle infrastructure funding and fleet electrification grants.
whyMatters: Federal grants can offset fleet transition costs for US road freight subcontractors. Understanding available programmes helps evaluate subcontractor investment timelines.

title: American Trucking Associations
note: Industry positions on EPA rules, clean truck programmes.
reasoning: Industry body positions signal how US trucking will respond to ZEV mandates.
whatIsIt: American Trucking Associations' environment programme covering industry response to EPA regulations and clean truck programmes.
whyMatters: ATA positions indicate how the US trucking industry will respond to regulations — important context for freight forwarders managing US subcontractor relationships.

title: DOT National Freight Strategic Plan
note: US multimodal freight policy including sustainability.
reasoning: Federal freight strategy sets priorities for infrastructure investment affecting routing decisions.
whatIsIt: US Department of Transportation's national strategic plan for multimodal freight including sustainability components.
whyMatters: Federal freight strategy influences infrastructure investment that affects routing options. Useful background for US freight planning.

title: EU CBAM
note: Carbon price on imported cement, steel, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen. Definitive phase from Jan 2026. Omnibus simplified: 50t de minimis, certificates from Feb 2027.
reasoning: CBAM definitive phase NOW — customs documentation and carbon costs on all covered EU imports. Direct operational impact for staging equipment containing steel/aluminium.
whatIsIt: EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism imposing carbon costs on imports of carbon-intensive goods entering the EU. Omnibus simplification adopted Oct 2025.
whyMatters: CBAM creates new customs documentation for EU-bound freight. Any shipment containing steel, aluminium, iron, cement, fertilisers, hydrogen, or electricity is affected. Only authorised CBAM declarants can import from Jan 2026. Freight forwarders must verify CBAM registration numbers before customs clearance.
keyData: Delegated acts on 3rd-country carbon price still pending

title: WTO Environment & Trade
note: WTO compatibility of carbon border measures; trade dispute monitoring.
reasoning: WTO challenges to CBAM could reshape global trade-linked climate rules.
whatIsIt: WTO's framework governing the intersection of trade rules and environmental measures like carbon border adjustments.
whyMatters: If CBAM faces WTO challenges, outcomes affect long-term trade flows. Freight forwarders need background intelligence on dispute trajectories affecting cross-border carbon pricing.

title: OECD Environment
note: Carbon pricing tracker across 70+ jurisdictions.
reasoning: Cross-jurisdictional carbon pricing comparison essential for route cost modelling.
whatIsIt: OECD's environmental division tracking carbon pricing instruments and effective carbon rates across 70+ jurisdictions.
whyMatters: Effective carbon rates vary dramatically by jurisdiction. This data helps freight forwarders compare routing options based on carbon cost exposure.

title: UNCTAD Sustainable Transport
note: How carbon pricing affects trade competitiveness and shipping costs.
reasoning: UN analysis of carbon pricing impacts on freight trade competitiveness.
whatIsIt: UN Conference on Trade and Development tracking how carbon pricing affects trade competitiveness and shipping cost structures.
whyMatters: UNCTAD analysis shows how carbon pricing differentials between jurisdictions create trade distortions that affect routing economics.

title: World Bank Carbon Pricing Dashboard
note: Live tracker of 73 carbon pricing instruments, 23% of global GHG.
reasoning: Global carbon price intelligence essential for forward pricing across all trade lanes.
whatIsIt: World Bank dashboard tracking all operational carbon pricing instruments worldwide — ETS markets and carbon taxes.
whyMatters: Carbon prices affect freight costs differently by jurisdiction. This is the essential tool for comparing carbon cost exposure across routing options and building forward pricing models.

title: ICAP ETS Map
note: International Carbon Action Partnership — comprehensive ETS tracker.
reasoning: Most detailed ETS status tracker globally — covers all operational and planned schemes.
whatIsIt: International Carbon Action Partnership providing comprehensive status tracking of emissions trading systems worldwide.
whyMatters: Detailed ETS status information for every jurisdiction helps freight forwarders understand where carbon costs are rising and plan accordingly.

title: GEF (Global Environment Facility)
note: Environmental financing for developing markets.
reasoning: Financing for developing-market carbon commitments affects long-term trade lane carbon costs.
whatIsIt: Multilateral financing mechanism for environmental projects in developing countries.
whyMatters: GEF-funded programmes in developing markets can create new environmental requirements on trade lanes used for event logistics.

title: CSRD
note: Mandatory ESG reporting. Post-Omnibus: 1,000+ employees mandatory, 250-1,000 voluntary opt-in. Scope 1/2/3 required. Assurance shifted from reasonable to limited.
reasoning: CSRD drives client data requests — even if forwarders are below threshold, clients in scope demand Scope 3 transport data.
whatIsIt: EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive requiring comprehensive sustainability disclosure including value chain emissions. Omnibus simplification adopted Feb 2026.
whyMatters: Even if a freight forwarder falls below the 1,000-employee Omnibus threshold, major clients subject to CSRD will require Scope 3 transport emissions data from logistics partners. The voluntary opt-in for 250-1,000 employee companies means some mid-size clients may CHOOSE to report, creating data requests even from below-threshold companies.

title: EU Taxonomy
note: Classification of 'green' economic activities. Transport categories defined.
reasoning: Taxonomy alignment unlocks green financing and client sustainable supply chain claims.
whatIsIt: EU classification system defining which economic activities qualify as environmentally sustainable for investment and disclosure.
whyMatters: If freight operations qualify as taxonomy-aligned, this unlocks green financing and allows clients to count logistics services toward their sustainable supply chain targets.

title: GRI Standards
note: Most widely used voluntary ESG framework globally.
reasoning: Baseline for CSRD-aligned reporting — many clients already report using GRI.
whatIsIt: Global Reporting Initiative standards — the most widely adopted voluntary sustainability reporting framework worldwide.
whyMatters: Many clients already use GRI for sustainability reporting. Understanding GRI indicators helps freight forwarders prepare data in formats clients need.
keyData: Most widely used ESG framework globally

title: ISO 14083
note: International standard for transport chain GHG emissions. Effective 2023.
reasoning: ISO 14083 is becoming the mandatory calculation methodology referenced by regulation and tenders.
whatIsIt: International standard providing methodology for calculating and reporting GHG emissions from transport chain operations.
whyMatters: ISO 14083 is the calculation methodology behind GLEC Framework and increasingly referenced in EU regulation. Freight forwarders must ensure emissions calculations are compliant for client reporting and regulatory requirements.

title: GLEC Framework v3
note: Industry standard for logistics emissions. Used for Scope 3 Cat 4 reporting.
reasoning: The practical methodology freight forwarders actually use for shipment-level emissions calculations.
whatIsIt: Smart Freight Centre's Global Logistics Emissions Council framework — the industry standard calculation methodology for logistics emissions.
whyMatters: GLEC is the tool event logistics specialists actually use to calculate shipment-level emissions. Clients increasingly specify GLEC-compliant reporting in tenders and RFPs. Essential for competitive positioning.

title: GHG Protocol
note: Foundation for all corporate carbon accounting. Scope 1/2/3.
reasoning: Category 4 (upstream transport) makes freight forwarders a Scope 3 source for virtually all clients.
whatIsIt: WRI/WBCSD standard defining Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions categories — the foundation for all corporate carbon accounting.
whyMatters: GHG Protocol Category 4 (Upstream transportation) is exactly what freight forwarders provide. Every client doing Scope 3 reporting needs logistics partners to supply emissions data in GHG Protocol format.

title: SBTi
note: Corporate net-zero target validation. Transport sector guidance.
reasoning: SBTi targets appear in client tenders — forwarders must understand methodology to respond credibly.
whatIsIt: Science Based Targets initiative validating corporate emissions reduction targets aligned with climate science.
whyMatters: Client RFPs increasingly require logistics partners to hold or be working toward SBTi-validated targets. Understanding the methodology is essential for credible tender responses.

title: ISSB IFRS S2
note: Global climate disclosure baseline. 20+ jurisdictions mandating.
reasoning: ISSB adoption globally means converging disclosure demands from clients in all jurisdictions.
whatIsIt: IFRS Sustainability Standards Board's climate disclosure standard being adopted by 20+ jurisdictions as mandatory reporting baseline.
whyMatters: As jurisdictions worldwide adopt ISSB, freight forwarders face converging data requests from clients globally — not just in the EU. Data systems must be globally interoperable.

title: CDP Supply Chain
note: 700+ purchasing orgs requesting supplier emissions data. Forwarders scored.
reasoning: CDP is the primary channel through which clients formally request emissions data from logistics suppliers.
whatIsIt: CDP's programme through which purchasing organisations formally request environmental performance data from their suppliers.
whyMatters: Over 700 purchasing organisations use CDP to request data from suppliers. Freight forwarders increasingly receive direct CDP questionnaires or must provide data that clients report through CDP.

title: EcoVadis
note: Supplier sustainability scorecards. Freight forwarder ratings affect contracts.
reasoning: EcoVadis scores directly gate contract eligibility with major shippers and event companies.
whatIsIt: Sustainability rating platform used by global brands to assess and score supplier ESG performance.
whyMatters: EcoVadis scores directly affect contract eligibility. Major event companies and brands require minimum scores from logistics suppliers. A poor rating can mean losing business.

title: EcoVadis Blog
note: Methodology changes, regulatory impacts on supply chain ESG.
reasoning: Tracks methodology updates that change how freight forwarders are scored.
whatIsIt: Updates on supplier sustainability rating trends, methodology changes, and regulatory impacts.
whyMatters: Methodology changes can shift scoring criteria. Staying current helps freight forwarders maintain or improve their rating.

title: EU Fit for 55 Package
note: Umbrella of 13 laws: ETS, CBAM, ReFuelEU, FuelEU, truck CO2 standards.
reasoning: Parent package of every major EU climate regulation affecting freight — the single reference point.
whatIsIt: EU's umbrella legislative package of 13 interconnected measures targeting 55% GHG reduction by 2030.
whyMatters: This is the parent package containing ETS expansion, CBAM, ReFuelEU Aviation, FuelEU Maritime, and truck CO2 standards. Every major EU regulation affecting freight traces back here.
keyData: Parent of ETS, CBAM, FuelEU, ReFuelEU

title: EU PPWR 2025/40
note: Replaces 1994 Directive. All packaging recyclable by 2030. Single-use restrictions.
reasoning: Affects ALL EU shipment packaging — every carton, pallet wrap, protective material from Aug 2026.
whatIsIt: EU regulation replacing the 1994 Packaging Directive with directly applicable rules on recyclability, reuse, and material restrictions.
whyMatters: Every piece of packaging on every EU-bound shipment must comply — cartons, pallet wrap, protective materials, crating. Non-compliant packaging may be rejected at EU borders. Critical for all freight sectors importing to the EU.

title: EEA (European Environment Agency)
note: EU environmental data portal; transport emission trends.
reasoning: Official EU environmental data — policy progress tracking for transport sector.
whatIsIt: EU agency providing environmental data, transport emission trends, and policy implementation tracking.
whyMatters: Official data source for EU transport emission trends and policy progress — useful for client briefings and benchmarking.

title: EUR-Lex
note: Official EU legislation database — primary source for all regulations.
reasoning: Daily EU law publication catches new delegated acts before industry sources.
whatIsIt: Official Journal of the European Union and full legislation database — the authoritative source for all EU law.
whyMatters: Fastest official source for new EU environmental regulations. RSS monitoring catches delegated acts, implementing measures, and amendments as they publish.

title: European Clean Trucking Alliance
note: EU ZEV truck advocacy; legislative progress tracking.
reasoning: Tracks EU legislative progress on heavy-duty vehicle standards.
whatIsIt: Coalition advocating for zero-emission truck standards in the EU; tracks legislative progress.
whyMatters: Useful for monitoring EU truck regulation development and policy signals.

title: UK DfT Decarbonisation
note: UK post-Brexit transport decarbonisation; independent SAF mandate, ZEV targets.
reasoning: Independent UK regulatory path post-Brexit means separate compliance requirements.
whatIsIt: UK Department for Transport's decarbonisation policy including independent SAF mandate and ZEV targets post-Brexit.
whyMatters: Post-Brexit UK has its own transport decarbonisation path. Freight forwarders serving UK events face separate compliance requirements from the EU.

title: Germany BMDV
note: German federal transport; hydrogen corridors, LNG/NH3 shipping.
reasoning: Germany leads EU on hydrogen corridors — signals infrastructure investment.
whatIsIt: German federal transport ministry leading on hydrogen corridor development and alternative maritime fuel infrastructure.
whyMatters: Germany's hydrogen corridor investments affect infrastructure availability for green freight across Central Europe.

title: EPA SmartWay
note: US freight carrier certification. Required by major shippers. 3,500+ carriers.
reasoning: Required by many US shippers for carrier qualification — effectively mandatory for US freight contracts.
whatIsIt: EPA's voluntary freight carrier environmental certification programme used by major US shippers for carrier selection.
whyMatters: SmartWay certification is effectively mandatory for major US freight contracts. Over 3,500 carriers enrolled. Event logistics operators need certification to serve US shippers requiring it.

title: Sustainable Packaging Coalition
note: US packaging sustainability standards; How2Recycle label.
reasoning: US packaging standards for event merchandise and goods — How2Recycle label increasingly required.
whatIsIt: US-led industry coalition setting packaging sustainability standards including the How2Recycle labelling system.
whyMatters: How2Recycle labels are increasingly required on event merchandise and goods packaging entering the US market.

title: NREL Transportation
note: US DOE lab: ZEV trucks, hydrogen, SAF, freight electrification R&D.
reasoning: National lab R&D signals which green freight technologies will become commercially viable.
whatIsIt: US National Renewable Energy Laboratory's transportation research covering zero-emission trucks, hydrogen, SAF, and freight electrification.
whyMatters: NREL research signals which green freight technologies will reach commercial viability and when — essential for infrastructure planning.

title: CEC North American Env Policy
note: Canada-Mexico-US trilateral environmental cooperation.
reasoning: Trilateral cooperation affects cross-border freight environmental standards.
whatIsIt: Commission for Environmental Cooperation — Canada-Mexico-US trilateral body for environmental policy coordination.
whyMatters: Cross-border environmental standards affect USMCA freight operations.

title: ECLAC (UN Latin America)
note: UN ECLAC transport logistics data for Latin America.
reasoning: LatAm logistics data for event freight routing through Brazil, Chile, Mexico.
whatIsIt: UN Economic Commission for Latin America tracking transport logistics data across the region.
whyMatters: Critical data source for event freight routing through Latin American markets.

title: Brazil Logística Reversa
note: Mandatory take-back for packaging, electronics, event materials.
reasoning: Brazil reverse logistics law directly affects event freight packaging and equipment entering Brazil.
whatIsIt: Brazil's reverse logistics law requiring mandatory take-back programmes for packaging, electronics, and specified materials.
whyMatters: Mandatory take-back applies to packaging, electronics, and industrial materials entering Brazil. Freight forwarders must ensure compliance documentation for all sectors importing to the Brazilian market.

title: Mexico SEMARNAT
note: Mexico environment ministry; packaging, carbon market, transport.
reasoning: Mexico environmental requirements affect cross-border event freight packaging and customs.
whatIsIt: Mexico's environmental ministry overseeing packaging regulations, carbon market development, and transport emissions rules.
whyMatters: Mexican environmental requirements affect packaging and customs compliance for cross-border event freight.

title: Colombian Ministry of Transport
note: Colombia transport policy for Andean event freight.
reasoning: Relevant for Andean event freight routing and last-mile compliance.
whatIsIt: Colombian ministry overseeing transport policy including sustainability requirements.
whyMatters: Relevant for event freight routing through Colombia and the Andean region.

title: IDB Sustainable LatAm Transport
note: IDB green freight corridor funding in Latin America.
reasoning: Development bank financing for green freight corridors in LatAm event markets.
whatIsIt: Inter-American Development Bank sustainable transport financing including green freight corridor development.
whyMatters: IDB-financed green corridors in LatAm can improve infrastructure for event freight operations.

title: MPA Singapore Green Shipping
note: Singapore green fuel bunkering, port incentives. World's largest bunkering hub.
reasoning: World's largest bunkering hub — green shipping programme sets standards for Asia-Pacific routing.
whatIsIt: Singapore Maritime and Port Authority's Green Shipping Programme offering incentives for clean fuel bunkering at the world's largest bunkering hub.
whyMatters: Singapore is the primary Asia-Pacific routing hub for ocean freight. MPA's green shipping incentives and bunkering availability directly affect vessel selection and routing decisions for event cargo to/from Asia.

title: Japan MLIT
note: Japan transport: shipping decarbonisation, hydrogen port strategy.
reasoning: Japan's hydrogen port strategy affects event logistics for Tokyo/Osaka destinations.
whatIsIt: Japan Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism — shipping decarbonisation and hydrogen port strategy.
whyMatters: Japan's hydrogen port investments and shipping decarbonisation policies affect freight logistics for one of Asia's largest trade destinations.

title: South Korea MOF
note: K-ETS includes shipping; ammonia/hydrogen vessel development.
reasoning: K-ETS carbon costs affect freight operations for Korean trade destinations.
whatIsIt: South Korea Ministry of Oceans and Fisheries — K-ETS shipping inclusion and zero-emission vessel development.
whyMatters: South Korea's carbon market includes shipping, and their vessel development programme signals future fuel availability.

title: Singapore Green Plan 2030
note: Singapore whole-of-government sustainability: maritime, aviation, packaging.
reasoning: Comprehensive sustainability roadmap covering all transport modes through Singapore hub.
whatIsIt: Singapore's comprehensive government sustainability roadmap covering maritime, aviation, packaging, and green buildings.
whyMatters: Covers sustainability requirements across all transport modes used by freight forwarders routing through Singapore.
keyData: Green building standards for event venues

title: ADB Sustainable Transport
note: ADB green transport financing across Southeast Asia.
reasoning: Development bank financing affects infrastructure quality for event freight in SE Asia.
whatIsIt: Asian Development Bank financing green transport infrastructure across Southeast Asia.
whyMatters: ADB-financed transport infrastructure improves freight capacity and sustainability in SE Asian event markets.

title: China CCICED
note: Tracks China's carbon policy trajectory.
reasoning: China's carbon policy evolution affects global shipping and manufacturing supply chains.
whatIsIt: China Council for International Cooperation on Environment and Development — advisory body tracking China's environmental policy.
whyMatters: China's carbon policy trajectory affects global shipping patterns and manufacturing supply chains relevant to event equipment sourcing.

title: Australia Climate Change Authority
note: Australian climate policy; Pacific event routing.
reasoning: Relevant for Pacific event routing and Australia/NZ freight compliance.
whatIsIt: Australian government advisory body on climate change policy.
whyMatters: Relevant for freight compliance when routing through Australia and the Pacific for events.

title: ASEAN Transport Strategic Plan
note: ASEAN sustainable transport across 10 member states.

title: IMO Net-Zero Framework
note: Global fuel standard + GHG pricing for ships >5,000 GT. Approved MEPC 83 (63-16-24). Adoption Oct 2025, entry into force Mar 2027, enforcement 2028. US opposes.
reasoning: The single most consequential maritime regulation since MARPOL. Mandatory fuel standard + carbon pricing on every ocean shipment. US actively opposing — creates enforcement fragmentation risk.
whatIsIt: First binding framework combining mandatory GHG fuel intensity limits and a global carbon pricing mechanism for international shipping. Approved by majority vote at MEPC 83 in April 2025.
whyMatters: Every ocean shipment will face fuel standard compliance costs passed through by carriers. The pricing mechanism creates a new cost layer separate from ETS. US opposition means carriers on US-origin routes may face different enforcement, creating pricing divergence clients must understand.
keyData: US walked out, formally opposes as 'global carbon tax'
IMO Net-Zero Fund for revenue disbursement

title: EU ICS2 (Import Control System 2)
note: Mandatory advance cargo data (ENS) for all EU-bound shipments. Operational now. All carriers and forwarders must submit Electronic Entry Summary before goods arrive.
reasoning: Operational NOW. Every EU-bound shipment requires advance ENS submission. Directly changes your daily customs workflow for every client.
whatIsIt: EU Import Control System requiring all carriers and freight forwarders to submit detailed Electronic Entry Summary Declarations before goods arrive at EU borders.
whyMatters: Every shipment to the EU requires compliant ENS data BEFORE arrival. Errors detected upstream cause delays across all cargo types. Freight forwarders are now information managers, not just transport organisers. Data quality is operationally critical.
keyData: Mandatory ENS for ALL EU-bound cargo
All carriers and forwarders must comply
Links to CBAM registration number from Jan 2026

title: EUDR (EU Deforestation Regulation)
note: Due diligence on 7 commodities (timber, rubber, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soy, cattle). Large operators from Dec 2026. Geolocation traceability required.
reasoning: Affects any client shipping timber, rubber, leather, or palm-oil-derived packaging into/within EU. Freight forwarders verify documentation compliance.
whatIsIt: EU regulation requiring companies to prove products entering the EU market are not linked to deforestation. Covers timber, rubber, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soy, and cattle derivatives.
whyMatters: Directly affects freight forwarders handling: timber crating, rubber components, leather goods, palm-oil-derived packaging materials, and wooden pallets. Forwarders must verify Due Diligence Statements accompany shipments containing covered commodities. Penalties up to 4% of EU-wide turnover.

title: CountEmissions EU
note: EU harmonised methodology for measuring transport GHG emissions. Built on ISO 14083. Will make emissions accounting legally referenced across EU.
reasoning: The regulation that makes ISO 14083/GLEC legally binding in the EU. Every freight quote will eventually need emissions calculation using this methodology.
whatIsIt: Proposed EU regulation establishing a harmonised methodology for GHG accounting of transport services, built on ISO 14083.
whyMatters: This regulation transforms emissions accounting from voluntary to legally referenced across all EU transport. Every freight quotation will need compliant emissions figures. Freight forwarders offering GLEC/ISO 14083 calculations now have a competitive advantage.
keyData: Applies 42 months after entry into force

title: FIATA
note: Global federation of freight forwarders. CBAM compliance resources, CO₂ calculators, regulatory alerts for the forwarding industry.
reasoning: Your industry's peak body. FIATA provides CBAM compliance tools, CO₂ calculators, and regulatory interpretation specifically for freight forwarders.
whatIsIt: International Federation of Freight Forwarders Associations — the global peak body representing the freight forwarding and logistics industry.
whyMatters: FIATA provides compliance resources built specifically for freight forwarders, not general industry. Their CBAM guidance, CO₂ calculator repository, and regulatory alerts are tailored to forwarding operations across all cargo sectors.

title: ICCT
note: Independent research on vehicle, marine, and aviation emissions. Most-cited source in EU regulatory impact assessments.
reasoning: The most-cited independent research organisation in freight emissions policy. Referenced by virtually every EU regulatory impact assessment.
whatIsIt: International Council on Clean Transportation providing independent research and analysis on vehicle, marine, and aviation emissions to regulators worldwide.
whyMatters: ICCT research underpins the data behind EU truck standards, maritime regulations, and aviation rules. Their projections inform the timeline expectations for every transport regulation in this dashboard.
keyData: Referenced in EU, US, China policy

title: Maritime Carbon Intelligence
note: Weekly briefing on maritime carbon economy: IMO, EU ETS, FuelEU, green fuels, carbon pricing.
reasoning: Specialized weekly briefing focused specifically on maritime carbon economy. Highest signal-to-noise for ocean shipping ESG.
whatIsIt: Specialized weekly intelligence briefing covering the maritime carbon economy including IMO regulations, EU ETS shipping, FuelEU Maritime, and green fuel markets.
whyMatters: Purpose-built for tracking the financial and regulatory dimensions of maritime decarbonisation. Directly relevant for pricing ocean freight surcharges across all cargo sectors.
